h2h_summary: Colombia and DR Congo have never previously met in any competitive or friendly international fixture, making their 2026 World Cup clash a historic first encounter.
wc_history_home: Colombia have appeared at six World Cups, with their best result being a quarter-final run in 2014 where James Rodriguez won the Golden Boot, and they missed out entirely in 2022.
wc_history_away: DR Congo (competing as Zaire) made their only previous World Cup appearance in 1974, losing all three group games including a 9-0 thrashing by Yugoslavia, and are back for the first time in 52 years.
group_context: Group K features Portugal as clear favourites, with Colombia widely expected to claim second place ahead of debutants Uzbekistan and DR Congo; Colombia need a win here to secure progression, while DR Congo are targeting an upset to keep their knockout hopes alive.
strengths_home: World-class attacking duo in Luis Diaz and James Rodriguez capable of unlocking any defence
Defensive solidity under Nestor Lorenzo — conceded only 15 goals in 18 CONMEBOL qualifiers
Strong recent form including 4-0 vs Mexico, 3-0 vs Australia, and 3-1 vs Costa Rica
strengths_away: Highly organised and compact defensively — conceded only 7 goals in 10 qualifying matches
Dangerous on the counter through Wissa's pace and Bakambu's experience and aerial presence
Fresh motivation and team unity as a historic second World Cup appearance after a 52-year absence
team_style_home: Colombia play a 4-2-3-1 with a double pivot shielding the defence, James Rodriguez as a free creative No.10, and Luis Diaz leading the press and attack from the left, emphasising technical possession and quick transitions.
team_style_away: DR Congo deploy a disciplined 4-2-3-1 / 4-3-3 under Sebastien Desabre that prioritises a compact defensive mid-block, selective high pressing, and rapid counter-attacking transitions through the pace of Yoane Wissa.
recent_news: Colombia should seal their place in the last 32 with victory over DR Congo in Guadalajara but the South Americans are unlikely to be in for a straightforward evening. Luis Diaz has scored two goals for Colombia in his last three international games, including their first match at the World Cup.
key_players_away: Aaron Wan-Bissaka (right back / West Ham United)
context: Group K features Portugal as clear favourites, with Colombia widely expected to claim second place ahead of debutants Uzbekistan and DR Congo; Colombia need a win here to secure progression, while DR Congo are targeting an upset to keep their knockout hopes alive.
